change: copyeditor.md Risk Mitigation Design — added the machine-written-drift rule (surface a count drift only when an author can edit one side; when both sides are generated the defect belongs to the generator, pinned by a regression test)
held_in_delta: motivating probe: Control 1.0 defect -> Treatment 0.0 (Control additionally proposed a tools/lint.py check comparing two machine-generated counts; Treatment explicitly declined it)
held_out_delta: over-fire canary (both sides author-editable): Control 1.0 -> Treatment 0.0 (Control proposed moving the count into an AUTO block via build.py; Treatment declined to touch the generator)
rationale: Behavioral probe variant, N=2 blind judges (different models) unanimous on all 4 responses: >=1 improvement on the motivating slice AND no over-fire on held-out; Treatment strictly more precise on both slices.
held_in_sampled: overview-catalog membership divergence (motivating; the only instance of this mechanism in the corpus)
held_out_sampled: hub prose vs authored timeline header count drift (synthetic; constructed to hold the manifesting structure — both sides author-editable — because the live corpus has 0 timelines)
note: Measurement ran AFTER adoption (commit 056a242) at operator request, not before as the runbook prescribes. First probe round was voided: the prompt disclosed that both artifacts were auto-generated, handing over the insight under test, so the failure did not reproduce under Control; re-run with that disclosure removed.

mechanism: synthesis grounds its headline answer on a derived sibling page that itself cites nothing; Pythia asserted as open-data with no raw span (OSAID-validated != open-data)
same Pythia open-data assertion, uncited; is the page the synthesis cites as its ground
Pythia listed under proof-of-viability (open-data) without raw grounding
board-vote procedural argument attributed to Perens; raw attributes it to Sam Johnston, who appears on zero bundle pages
[forecast] claim tags Bradley Kuhn under [[FreeSoftwareFoundation]]; same page's summary says Software Freedom Conservancy — recurrence of the mechanism fixed on licensing-open-washing 2026-07-17
bare 'Meta's Llama' vs synthesis 'Llama 2'; OSI verdict is version-specific and the corpus discusses 3 Llama releases
'Eleuther AI' vs 'EleutherAI' in Mozilla.md — same org, two spellings across bundle
closes on 'the point to monitor is the OSI board election' (announced 2024-12); ~19 months and 2 election cycles later, no outcome recorded
cluster's definitional anchor carries zero contestation in narrator voice though case-against-osaid is in its own frontmatter sources

four derived pages rebut OSI's 'niche-only' defense, but that defense exists as a claim on no L2-1 page; same unrecorded-passage shape as the open-data blind spot. DEFERRED - not blocking, accurate where used
S3 first paragraph absorbed 3 ADAPT rounds: ~180 words, 6 sentences, 3 nested quote levels; reads as a ledger of corrections. DEFERRED - readability, not accuracy
hub last_updated MUST NOT has no detector — staleness.py excludes entity/concept from the body-dated set, hub_schema --fix only inserts a missing field
broken-link probe gates on '/wiki/' while the sibling stub advisory matches 'wiki/...' without a leading slash — a relative file_path fires one and not the other
stale-hub row carries why the old rating existed rather than a present-tense trigger; sits inside the operator-pending marker
